description: Repository-only technical watchlist for Ethereum protocol and implementation-facing semantic signals. It is excluded from generated public docs and is not a registry source of truth, pricing guide, buyer-targeting artifact, or commercial narrative.

claim_policy.pricing: Do not include pricing, price ranges, valuation language, buyer targeting, or sales language.
claim_policy.ethereum_foundation: Do not present Ethereum Foundation as a direct buyer.
claim_policy.public_narrative: Do not use this file to modify public narrative, public market pages, anchors, slugs, schemas, or registry structure.
claim_policy.mainnet_status: Do not claim ePBS is deployed on Ethereum mainnet unless independently verified in a future update.
claim_policy.fork_status: Do not claim FOCIL is included in Glamsterdam; current operational guidance treats it as not included for Glamsterdam.
claim_policy.evidence_split: Separate verifiable protocol or implementation evidence from non-registry interpretation; keep this file technical and non-commercial.
claim_policy.allowed_language: Prefer prudent language such as scheduled, implementation-facing, protocol-relevant, research-aligned, candidate signal, or watch-only.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Protocol-relevant and implementation-facing roadmap signal; do not claim Ethereum mainnet deployment.
entries.internal_notes: Track as an enshrined proposer-builder separation signal connected to builder/proposer role separation, payload commitments, and protocol-level PBS design.
entries.risk_notes: Do not imply deployed mainnet status, pricing relevance, buyer demand, or Ethereum Foundation purchasing intent.

entries.public_claim_allowed: May be described as protocol-relevant terminology aligned with ePBS work; do not claim deployed status.
entries.internal_notes: Track as the expanded semantic form of ePBS for internal terminology matching and implementation-facing monitoring.
entries.risk_notes: Avoid treating the term as a public commercial narrative or proof of mainnet activation.

entries.public_claim_allowed: May be referenced as the long-form ePBS terminology; avoid mainnet deployment claims.
entries.internal_notes: Use for matching explicit long-form references to proposer-builder separation being moved into protocol design.
entries.risk_notes: Do not overstate maturity beyond implementation-facing roadmap signal.

entries.public_claim_allowed: May be treated as broad proposer-builder separation terminology; avoid conflating external PBS infrastructure with ePBS deployment.
entries.internal_notes: Broad parent term for builder/proposer separation, external PBS infrastructure, and enshrined PBS discussions.
entries.risk_notes: Term is broad; avoid using it as evidence for any specific fork inclusion, buyer segment, or pricing thesis.

entries.public_claim_allowed: May be used as general protocol-relevant terminology; qualify specific claims when discussing enshrined variants.
entries.internal_notes: Track lower-case generic usage for semantic matching across protocol, infrastructure, and research contexts.
entries.risk_notes: Do not equate every generic PBS mention with scheduled ePBS deployment.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Research-aligned and protocol-relevant candidate signal; do not claim Glamsterdam inclusion.
entries.internal_notes: Track fork-choice enforced inclusion lists as an inclusion-list design signal that current operational guidance treats as not included for Glamsterdam.
entries.risk_notes: Main risk is overstating roadmap status or implying scheduled inclusion despite current operational guidance.

entries.public_claim_allowed: May be tracked as the expanded FOCIL term; do not claim Glamsterdam inclusion.
entries.internal_notes: Use as the long-form matcher for FOCIL-related inclusion-list discussions.
entries.risk_notes: Avoid collapsing long-form FOCIL references into generic inclusion guarantees or scheduled fork content.

entries.public_claim_allowed: May be described as protocol-relevant inclusion-list terminology; qualify specific mechanisms and fork status.
entries.internal_notes: Generic inclusion-list terminology should remain separate from specific FOCIL claims and fork scheduling claims.
entries.risk_notes: Do not imply that all inclusion-list research maps to Glamsterdam or to a single EIP.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Internal watch-only semantic signal unless tied to a specific verified mechanism.
entries.internal_notes: Track as broader language around censorship resistance, inclusion constraints, and transaction inclusion assurances.
entries.risk_notes: Term can imply stronger guarantees than a specific proposal provides; avoid overclaiming.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Research-aligned role terminology; avoid claiming production or fork inclusion status.
entries.internal_notes: Track committee-role language associated with inclusion-list designs and implementation discussions.
entries.risk_notes: Do not present committee structure as finalized protocol architecture without verified evidence.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Technical execution-layer signal; do not frame as commercial demand or pricing input.
entries.internal_notes: Track as shorthand for Block-Level Access Lists and execution predictability or parallel execution discussions.
entries.risk_notes: Avoid confusing BAL/EIP-7928 with repricing or state-creation gas-cost changes.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Technical plural shorthand for Block-Level Access Lists; not a commercial claim.
entries.internal_notes: Track plural usage for search, classification, and internal semantic clustering.
entries.risk_notes: Do not attach pricing, buyer targeting, or broad market conclusions to plural BAL references.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Protocol-relevant execution-layer terminology; keep public claims technical and non-commercial.
entries.internal_notes: Track as the explicit technical form connected to block-level state access declaration, execution predictability, and parallel execution readiness.
entries.risk_notes: Do not conflate with EIP-8037 or present as evidence of buyer demand.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Technical EIP signal related to BAL tracking; avoid commercial or pricing interpretation.
entries.internal_notes: Use as the primary EIP reference for Block-Level Access Lists within this internal watchlist.
entries.risk_notes: Do not confuse with EIP-8037 repricing/state creation gas cost scope.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Technical EIP signal; do not describe as BAL.
entries.internal_notes: Track separately as repricing/state creation gas cost signal, not as Block-Level Access Lists.
entries.risk_notes: Primary risk is confusing EIP-8037 with BAL/EIP-7928 or using it for unsupported execution-market claims.

entries.public_claim_allowed: FOCIL-related EIP signal; do not claim Glamsterdam inclusion.
entries.internal_notes: Track as the FOCIL-related EIP signal under current operational guidance that treats FOCIL as not included for Glamsterdam.
entries.risk_notes: Avoid presenting declined-for-current-fork status as protocol rejection in all future contexts.

entries.public_claim_allowed: ePBS-related EIP signal; do not claim ePBS mainnet deployment.
entries.internal_notes: Track as the core ePBS-associated EIP signal for proposer-builder separation and payload-related semantics.
entries.risk_notes: Avoid using this EIP as proof of current mainnet deployment or direct buyer demand.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Internal technical watch signal unless tied to verified protocol status in a future update.
entries.internal_notes: Track as a meta-EIP and fork-context signal for Glamsterdam; keep usage technical and separate from commercial or pricing claims.
entries.risk_notes: Do not infer buyer relevance, pricing relevance, or public commercial narrative from this meta-EIP/fork-context signal.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Internal technical watch signal unless tied to verified protocol status in a future update.
entries.internal_notes: Track as a meta-EIP and fork-context signal for Hegotá and FOCIL; keep usage as internal roadmap context unless future verification supports public claims.
entries.risk_notes: Do not infer scheduling beyond internal fork-context tracking, buyer relevance, pricing relevance, or public narrative from this signal alone.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Technical role terminology; avoid treating roles as buyers or commercial targets.
entries.internal_notes: Track role-separation language across PBS, ePBS, payload construction, and proposer-builder coordination.
entries.risk_notes: Do not convert role terminology into buyer targeting or pricing assumptions.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Technical infrastructure surface; no confirmed buyer or pricing claim allowed.
entries.internal_notes: Track as infrastructure-adjacent language around relays, builders, validators, and proposer-builder workflows.
entries.risk_notes: Do not imply that infrastructure operators are buyers or that ePBS deployment status is confirmed.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Technical ecosystem surface only; avoid buyer, pricing, or public sales claims.
entries.internal_notes: Track as a broad ecosystem surface related to builders, relays, searchers, and block construction workflows.
entries.risk_notes: Term is broad and commercializable; keep watchlist usage technical and non-commercial.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Technical actor category; do not present as confirmed buyers.
entries.internal_notes: Track as protocol-adjacent operators relevant to validator duties, proposer responsibilities, and implementation impact.
entries.risk_notes: Avoid buyer targeting, Ethereum Foundation purchasing implications, or pricing assumptions.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Internal technical coordination surface; qualify carefully against specific PBS or ePBS designs.
entries.internal_notes: Track auction-framing language around execution rights, block building, and proposer-builder coordination.
entries.risk_notes: Do not transform auction terminology into commercial pricing or sale narrative.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Candidate signal for technical coordination analysis; avoid public commercial claims.
entries.internal_notes: Track as a broad abstraction for authority over execution payload construction, ordering, or related coordination decisions.
entries.risk_notes: Term is abstract; do not treat as an established protocol primitive or market claim without further evidence.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Technical execution-layer signal; connect to verified mechanisms only when evidence supports it.
entries.internal_notes: Track as an execution-performance and determinism signal that may relate to BAL-style state-access declarations.
entries.risk_notes: Do not claim BAL alone delivers a complete parallel execution roadmap or commercial demand.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Roadmap/fork-context signal only; do not claim FOCIL inclusion.
entries.internal_notes: Track as fork context for scheduled or implementation-facing signals while keeping FOCIL explicitly out under current operational guidance; FOCIL must not be presented as included in Glamsterdam.
entries.risk_notes: Avoid using fork context to imply inclusion of every watched EIP or research proposal.

entries.public_claim_allowed: Roadmap-context signal only; verify before public claims.
entries.internal_notes: Track as a fork-context/internal roadmap signal for Hegotá and FOCIL-related monitoring; do not treat as a public absolute claim without future verification.
entries.risk_notes: Do not infer pricing, buyer relevance, or EIP inclusion without future verification.
